_metadata.description: Maps CAMEO event codes (used by GDELT) to the 8-category geopolitical event taxonomy defined in Phase 1. Each entry specifies the taxonomy category, a confidence level for the mapping, and filtering notes. Codes not listed here are excluded from ingestion (diplomatic routine, cooperation, aid — not geopolitically significant for our use case). Validated against: Russia invasion of Ukraine (Feb 2022, CAMEO 190/193/194 → armed_conflict_instability), US tariffs on China (2025, requires GTA supplementation — GDELT CAMEO coverage of trade policy is poor).

confidence_levels.high: Direct, unambiguous mapping — CAMEO code reliably indicates this category
confidence_levels.medium: Context-dependent — apply additional GoldsteinScale or keyword filters
confidence_levels.low: Weak signal — use only when supplemented by other source confirmation

gdelt_filter_requirements.note: Apply NumMentions >= 10 to all codes. Apply GoldsteinScale <= -5 only where noted.

CAMEO_13.label: THREATEN (root)
CAMEO_13.note: Use subcodes below; root code alone is too broad

CAMEO_130.note: Generic threat — too ambiguous, skip without subcode

CAMEO_131.note: Non-specific threat (non-force); include only if GoldsteinScale <= -3

CAMEO_132.note: Accusations between states — monitor but low geopolitical signal

CAMEO_133.note: Ultimatum — precursor to conflict; require GoldsteinScale <= -5

CAMEO_134.note: Threat of administrative sanctions — often precedes actual sanctions

CAMEO_135.note: Threat of political sanctions — includes threatened asset freezes, entity listings

CAMEO_136.note: Threat of armed force — strong conflict precursor; require GoldsteinScale <= -7

CAMEO_137.note: CBRN threat — extreme severity; always include

CAMEO_138.note: Threat to boycott/embargo/stop aid — maps to trade policy when trade-related; cross-check with GTA

CAMEO_139.note: EXCLUDED: Threat to halt negotiations is too vague without treaty/alliance context, which GDELT cannot provide. Was generating noise.

CAMEO_140.note: Generic protest; require GoldsteinScale <= -3 and NumMentions >= 20

CAMEO_142.note: Hunger strike — low geopolitical signal unless state-level

CAMEO_144.note: Obstruct passage — blockades, border closures with physical obstruction

CAMEO_145.note: Violent protest/riot — maps to instability; require GoldsteinScale <= -5

CAMEO_150.note: Generic force posture — use subcodes where available

CAMEO_151.note: Increase police alert — low signal unless associated with civil unrest

CAMEO_152.note: Increase military alert — strong conflict signal

CAMEO_154.note: Military exercises — signal of tension; context-dependent

CAMEO_155.note: Halt military action — de-escalation signal; track as conflict end marker

CAMEO_160.note: EXCLUDED: Generic relation reduction is overwhelmingly diplomatic noise. 378k events at GoldsteinScale -3 to -4 with no source URLs. Use subcodes 161-165 only.

CAMEO_161.note: Reduce diplomatic relations — ambassador expulsions, downgrade of ties

CAMEO_162.note: Expel diplomatic staff — typically accompanies sanctions escalation

CAMEO_163.note: Suspend aid — economic coercion; maps to sanctions channel

CAMEO_164.note: Halt negotiations — breakdown of trade/alliance talks

CAMEO_165.note: Break relations entirely — severe realignment signal

CAMEO_166.note: Impose embargo or boycott — direct trade policy action

CAMEO_171.note: Impose embargo, boycott, or sanctions — PRIMARY sanctions mapping; cross-reference OFAC source
CAMEO_171.validation_note: Russia SWIFT exclusion (Feb 2022) should appear here

CAMEO_172.note: Blockade/restrict movement — maritime blockades, border closures; e.g. Red Sea attacks

CAMEO_173.note: State of emergency — domestic political instability signal

CAMEO_174.note: Administrative/political discrimination — could indicate regulatory sovereignty shift

CAMEO_175.note: Halt negotiations on trade — map to trade policy if trade-specific

CAMEO_176.note: Arrest/detain — political detention; relevant for business-person risk

CAMEO_181.note: Abduction/kidnapping — high severity, especially in conflict zones

CAMEO_184.note: Arson — typically lower severity unless infrastructure target

CAMEO_185.note: Attempted assassination — high political instability signal

CAMEO_186.note: Assassination — extreme political instability; always include

CAMEO_190.note: Generic fighting — always include; GoldsteinScale will be strongly negative
CAMEO_190.validation_note: Russia-Ukraine war (Feb 24, 2022) generates dense CAMEO 190/193/194 events

CAMEO_193.note: Aerial attack — air strikes, drone attacks

CAMEO_194.note: Engage in war — interstate armed conflict

CAMEO_201.note: Mass expulsion — population displacement; extreme conflict signal

CAMEO_202.note: Mass killing — atrocities; always include regardless of NumMentions

CAMEO_204.note: WMD use — maximum severity; always include

NOTES_ON_GDELT_COVERAGE_GAPS.trade_policy_actions: GDELT has poor coverage of trade policy events. Tariff announcements, trade agreement changes, and customs measures are rarely coded as distinct CAMEO events — they appear diffusely in 13x/17x codes but without specificity. Use Global Trade Alert as the primary source for this category.
NOTES_ON_GDELT_COVERAGE_GAPS.technology_controls: Export control announcements (BIS Entity List additions, chip export rules) are not captured in CAMEO. Use BIS and Federal Register scraping as primary sources.
NOTES_ON_GDELT_COVERAGE_GAPS.resource_energy_disruptions: OPEC decisions, commodity shocks, and energy weaponization appear in GDELT but inconsistently. Supplement with commodity price data and specialized energy news feeds.
NOTES_ON_GDELT_COVERAGE_GAPS.regulatory_sovereignty_shifts: FDI screening decisions, data localization laws, and local content requirements are essentially uncovered by CAMEO. Requires regulatory news feeds or manual ingestion.
NOTES_ON_GDELT_COVERAGE_GAPS.institutional_alliance_realignment: Treaty changes and multilateral org reforms appear in CAMEO 16x but with low signal-to-noise. Cross-reference with institutional news sources.
NOTES_ON_GDELT_COVERAGE_GAPS.sanctions_financial_restrictions: CAMEO 17x catches broad coercion but misses many specific sanctions. OFAC SDN list additions are the authoritative source — GDELT is secondary here.
